next_step_text: 6/29/26 GK: Christina follow up; closing FY end of June. asked to connect 3rd week of July. setting tentative connect 6/9/26 GK: Christina message - will connect with us end of month 5/26/26 GK: Christina response; working through internal approvals, will reach out with update shortly 5/19/26 GK: connecting with Christina 5/22 for project update 5/11/26 GK: follow up to full Pep Team 5/5/26 GK: em

Provide initial pricing estimate and finalize the quote with Christina
Plan and schedule an on-site Cincinnati hands-on session
Confirm with the integration team on integrating IT/security questionnaires

Christina and Tim to confirm dates and schedule on-site visit or extended POC
Christina to provide an update once the internal approvals process completes
Christina to reconnect third week of July 2026 after fiscal year-end close

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the strongest current threat is the internal build (extend Pep Connect), kept explicitly alive by Tim Drost on 17 Dec 2025 ('it still could happen') and backed by 20+ in-house developers; do-nothing/status-quo is the secondary risk given the stalled approvals; 'multiple vendors' were referenced but never named. We win on speed, freeing engineers for client-facing work, and native AI/Merlin depth no internal build can match.

competitors.name: Internal Build (extend Pep Connect system)
competitors.quote: We looked at building this, and for my comments earlier, it still could happen. We wanna focus on our clients and building stuff for our clients and not on this. Not that it's not built. It's just not, you know, I think you get it. So this is not like a warm and fuzzy let's learn more. We truly are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Dec 2025, Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Operations)
competitors.how_we_win: Speed to value (Zycus 10-20 weeks vs internal build 12-18 months), engineering focus (keep Pep's 20+ developers on client-facing AI and campaign features, not plumbing), and AI depth (Merlin risk scoring, OCR document extraction, clause intelligence, which Pep cannot build without heavy ML investment). Activate the wedge by building a quantified buy-vs-build business case (cost of internal build = dev hours + opportunity cost of not building client features + 12-18 month delay vs Zycus $120k + 10-20 week implementation + immediate ROI on manual-process elimination and exception-tracking automation), present it to the EB with Tim's executive sponsorship, and force a vendor-selection decision before third-week-of-July reconnect.

competitors.name: Other third-party vendor platforms (unnamed)
competitors.quote: We truly are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Dec 2025, Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Operations); SF Next_Step__c 17 Dec 2025 'evaluating multiple vendors'
competitors.how_we_win: No competitor names or sentiment visible across 4 calls and 7 months, which suggests either Pep is tight-lipped or Zycus is the only external platform being seriously evaluated. If other vendors exist, Zycus wins by demonstrating tighter fit to Pep's workflow (scenario session 13 Feb validated exact onboarding process), deeper AI capabilities (Merlin suite), and faster implementation. If Zycus is sole external platform, the real competition is the internal build, not another vendor.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / slow decision
competitors.quote: We also don't have a timeline... it's not urgent.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 17 Dec 2025, Christina Behm (Sourcing Operations Manager)
competitors.how_we_win: Create urgency by quantifying the cost of inaction (manual onboarding continues to block fast client engagements per Tim 17 Dec 'program launches in 3 weeks, forced to make exceptions'; exception tracking stays in buyers' heads, creating single-point-of-failure risk if Christina or Jillian leave; expired COIs and agreements create compliance and audit exposure per Jillian 17 Dec). Tie urgency to a business event (new fiscal year starting 1 Jul 2026, new client onboarding wave, compliance audit, leadership visibility into supplier risk). Without urgency, deal drifts indefinitely.

competitors.name: Internal build (extend Pep Connect)
competitors.quote: we looked at building this, and for my comments earlier, it still could happen
competitors.how_we_win: Tim wants engineering focus on client products, not the supplier arm; position Zycus speed-to-value plus native Merlin AI (redlining, risk radar) the internal team cannot build quickly

competitors.name: Other third-party vendors (unnamed)
competitors.quote: we are engaging a number of folks and gonna make a decision here on a path forward
competitors.how_we_win: Lock in the iSupplier/iContract/iRisk fit confirmed in the scenario session and convert it to a written differentiated proposal before competitors are named

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (Pep Connect as-is)
competitors.quote: it's not urgent. I don't have a timeline or a budget
competitors.how_we_win: Create a compelling event by quantifying exception/COI risk exposure and onboarding delay cost so inaction has a price.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (keep Pep Connect as-is)
competitors.quote: it's not urgent. I don't have a timeline or a budget
competitors.how_we_win: Tie a compelling event to rising exceptions and COI lapse risk; quantify the cost of manual tracking to create urgency

champion_strength.source: All four calls 21 Nov 2025 - 13 Feb 2026; SF Next_Step__c
champion_strength.summary: Christina Behm is a genuine working champion but mid-level and now in an approvals holding pattern; no secondary champion and the higher-power VP (Tim Drost) is disengaged.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer engaged. Tim Drost (VP Sourcing Ops, Christina's manager) validated need on 17 Dec 2025 and is the closest budget signal, but has not driven the deal since and is unconfirmed as budget holder. The $120k has sat in an opaque 'internal approvals' process since 26 May. Arming Christina to broker a Tim conversation — not cold-bypassing her — is the path to power. Until a budget owner validates the spend, the Upside Key Deal forecast is indefensible.

recommended_moves: Build a one-page quantified value case (cost of manual exception tracking, COI/insurance lapse exposure, onboarding-delay cost) and arm Christina with it to carry into her internal approval — position it explicitly as buy-vs-build (Zycus 10-20 weeks + Merlin AI vs an open-ended internal project that pulls 20+ devs off client work).
Ask Christina to broker a short working session with Tim Drost to confirm budget ownership and sponsor the value case — route through the champion, do not cold-outreach the EB.
Send the finalized iSupplier/iContract/iRisk quote and lock an on-site/POC date as a forcing event.
